title: Assembly Elections 2026: How to vote from home for elderly and disabled
content: Senior citizens aged 85 years and above and Persons with Disabilities (PwDs) who have a minimum 40% benchmark disability (as certified by a medical authority) are eligible to vote from home

title: Four queer voices explain the impact of the Trans Amendment Bill 2026

They strongly call for repealing the amendment

title: Delhi Cops Crack Down On Arms Smuggling Network Linked To Pakistan, Bangladesh

title: 'Adequate stock available': Govt issues clarification on LPG refill booking timeline
content: Government has debunked false reports circulating on social media about revised LPG refill booking timelines.  Existing booking periods of 25 days in urban and 45 days in rural areas remain unchanged.  Authorities assure sufficient fuel stocks amidst Middle East tensions and urge the public to avoid panic buying and misinformation.

title: 'Anti-Bengali party': Mamata vs BJP over puja pandal permission row
content: As West Bengal assembly elections approach, the BJP and TMC are trading sharp accusations. BJP chief Nitin Nabin alleged Hindus need court permission for puja pandals while Muslims can offer namaz freely. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee countered, calling the BJP an 'anti-Bengali' party and raising concerns about voter roll deletions and citizenship.

title: 'Shameful to summon 3-yr old': SC raps Haryana Police over 'insensitive' handling of child rape case
content: The Supreme Court has sharply criticized Haryana Police and the Child Welfare Committee for their insensitive handling of a three-year-old rape survivor's case. The court condemned summoning the minor to the police station and expressed concern over the apparent downgrading of charges under the Pocso Act, emphasizing the need for a child-sensitive approach.

title: Dinner invite, hidden gun: How Ghaziabad man shot wife, her lover; leaves 7 children behind
content: A man in Ghaziabad allegedly murdered his wife and her alleged lover after confronting them over an affair. The accused, Rashid, reportedly shot his wife Shabnam and friend Faheem after a dinner at their home. Their seven-year-old son witnessed the killings before the father fled.

title: Elon Musk shares when he thought he had less than 10% chances of surviving
content: SpaceX is reportedly preparing for a massive IPO, potentially raising over $75 billion, a remarkable feat considering its near-bankruptcy in 2008.  Elon Musk, reflecting on the company's early struggles, revealed the immense emotional toll, stating he believed survival chances were less than 10%. This journey from near-failure to a historic public offering highlights incredible perseverance.

title: ‘Dhurandhar: The Revenge’ box office day 7 live: Ranveer Singh starrer chases Rs 700 cr
content: 'Dhurandhar: The Revenge' featuring Ranveer Singh and directed by Aditya Dhar has cemented its status as an all-time blockbuster, crossing the monumental Rs. 900 crore gross milestone worldwide in its first week. Despite a massive 3-hour and 49-minute runtime, the espionage thriller has maintained an iron grip on the domestic market, fueled by strong word-of-mouth and festive boosts from Ugadi and Gudi Padwa. From its record-breaking Rs. 114.85 crore peak on Day 4 to its current global gross of Rs. 919 crore, 'Dhurandhar 2' continues to shatter records as the fastest Indian film to breach the Rs. 500 crore mark.

title: Rinku Singh appointed Regional Sports Officer: Salary, role explained
content: Rinku Singh received a dual honor, being appointed vice-captain of KKR for IPL 2026 and then appointed as a Regional Sports Officer by the Uttar Pradesh Government. CM Yogi Adityanath presented him with the appointment letter, highlighting UP's commitment to prioritizing athletes in government service. This new role will see Rinku shaping the future of sports in Uttar Pradesh.

title: Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas wants everyone to believe that AI layoffs are not so bad
content: Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas offers an optimistic outlook on AI's impact on jobs, viewing potential layoffs as an opportunity for entrepreneurship. He highlights AI's ability to empower small businesses and individuals to launch ventures with minimal resources, citing examples of successful AI-driven startups.  This perspective contrasts with growing concerns about widespread AI-induced unemployment.

title: Nayara plans 35-day shutdown, 8% of India's refining capacity may take a hit
content: A large portion of the refinery’s output is sold within the domestic market, with exports having declined after the sanctions last year. A considerable share of production is supplied to state-run refiners.

title: 'Dressing up defeat as agreement': Iran mocks Trump with 'negotiating with yourself' dig
content: An Iranian military spokesperson has ridiculed US ceasefire efforts, dismissing Donald Trump's claims of ongoing discussions and stating Iran will never make a deal with the US. The remarks come as the US ramps up its military presence in the region, despite ongoing airstrikes and missile attacks.

title: Iran war impact: Philippines declares energy emergency with only 40 days of fuel remaining
content: The Philippines has declared a national energy emergency due to escalating Middle East conflict, fearing imminent danger to fuel supplies. President Marcos Jr. warned of potential flight groundings and industry rationing. The government is empowered to enforce conservation, fast-track procurement, and provide commuter relief. This move highlights the nation's vulnerability to global oil disruptions.

title: OnePlus India CEO Robin Liu steps down, company gives update on India operations
content: OnePlus India CEO Robin Liu has stepped down amidst reports of a global business restructuring. The company confirmed Liu's departure, thanking him for his contributions and wishing him well. OnePlus India operations will continue with a local strategy, ensuring business continuity.

title: 'Pre-planned massacre’: Bangladesh marks Genocide Day, slams Pakistan over 1971
content: In a solemn observance of Genocide Day, Bangladesh's Prime Minister Tarique Rahman has expressed his profound condemnation of the atrocious acts committed by the Pakistani military during Operation Searchlight on March 25, 1971. He referred to this tragic event, where countless unarmed civilians were mercilessly targeted, as an indelible stain on history

title: Who is Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr? The hard‑line commander now shaping Iran’s security
content: Following Ali Larijani's death, Iran appointed former IRGC commander Mohammad Bagher Zolghadr as the new chief of the Supreme National Security Council. Zolghadr's extensive military and political background, including his IRGC leadership, signals a consolidation of the Revolutionary Guard's influence in key security and foreign policy decisions.

title: Bomb threat to Delhi assembly again: Email claims 16 RDX IEDs planted, blast warning
content: The Delhi Vidhan Sabha received a bomb threat via email on Wednesday, alleging 16 RDX-laden IEDs were planted with a planned explosion at 1:40 PM. The sender cited political grievances, prompting immediate evacuation and deployment of security agencies for thorough checks. An investigation is underway to trace the email's origin and verify the threat's authenticity.

title: FB ‘friend’ turns killer: Parlour owner hacked to death in Kolkata; attacker dies by suicide
content: A 50-year-old beauty parlour owner was found hacked to death in her Garia establishment on Tuesday. The assailant, a 44-year-old man believed to be from Haryana or Kashmir, was discovered dead outside, with police suspecting he died by suicide. Investigators believe a relationship gone sour was the motive.

title: 'Your yorker is breathtaking': Shami breathes fire, stumps shattered
content: Veteran pacer Mohammed Shami, 35, showcased lethal yorkers in Lucknow Super Giants' nets, impressing coach Justin Langer. Bowling coach Bharat Arun expressed excitement about working with Shami again, highlighting his significant contribution to India's past overseas Test victories. LSG's pace attack, a blend of experience and emerging talent, faces the challenge of managing workloads post-injury.

title: 'No shortage of petrol, diesel, or LPG': Oil firms soothe fuel concerns in India
content: Major oil companies have debunked claims of a fuel shortage, assuring the public that supplies are normal and sufficient. Despite global supply tensions due to the Middle East crisis, firms like BPCL, HPCL, and Indian Oil emphasize no panic buying is needed. They are committed to uninterrupted service, with real-time updates confirming smooth operations at retail outlets.

title: How Iran war is helping Kim Jong Un justify North Korea's nuclear arsenal
content: North Korean leader Kim Jong Un defended his nation's nuclear arsenal, citing the ongoing Iran war as justification for strong military deterrence. He accused the US of "terrorism and invasions" and vowed to advance North Korea's "defensive nuclear deterrence." Kim also declared South Korea the "most hostile nation," warning of severe consequences for any provocations.

title: Closure of American bases in Gulf, reparation for attacks: What Iran is demanding in truce deal
content: Escalating Middle East tensions see Iran setting high demands for a ceasefire with the US, including base closures and reparations. The US, meanwhile, has proposed a 15-point plan via Pakistan, focusing on nuclear and missile programs, with a potential one-month ceasefire and sanctions relief.

title: In the shadow of war: How US long-range 'B-2 Spirit' strikes deep inside Iran
content: Amidst escalating Middle East tensions, the B-2 Spirit stealth bomber is proving indispensable against Iran. Operating directly from Missouri, this advanced aircraft penetrates sophisticated defenses, showcasing its unparalleled strategic reach. Images reveal B-2s launching for critical long-range missions, underscoring its vital role in global power projection and precision strikes.

title: Stock market: Nifty50 goes above 23,400; BSE Sensex jumps 1,500 points as oil prices cool
content: Stock market today: Nifty50 and BSE Sensex continued their rally on Wednesday rising over 1.5% in early morning trade. While Nifty50 went above 23,250, BSE Sensex rose over 1,100 points.

title: Can Type 2 diabetes begin in childhood? What parents must know
content: Type 2 diabetes is alarming doctors as it's increasingly diagnosed in Indian children and teens, often linked to rising childhood obesity. Lifestyle changes, including increased screen time and poor diet, are fueling this trend. Early detection and parental intervention are crucial to combat this growing health crisis and prevent long-term complications.

title: Ram Navami 2026: Date, shubh muhurat, history & puja vidhi
content: Lord Shri Ram's birth anniversary, Ram Navami, is celebrated with immense joy on March 26, 2026. This significant Hindu festival commemorates the divine manifestation of Lord Ram, an embodiment of righteousness. Devotees engage in prayers, religious recitations, and visit Ayodhya to honor the revered deity, seeking his blessings.

title: Why Virat Kohli turned bowler at RCB nets
content: Virat Kohli was spotted mentoring Phil Salt in a recent RCB practice session, offering batting tips as the English opener aims to bounce back from a dip in form.

title: Gold price prediction: Where is gold headed today & in the near-term?
content: Gold price prediction today: Gold prices are likely to see movement based on inflation projections and rate cut expectations, says Maneesh Sharma, AVP - Commodities & Currencies at Anand Rathi Shares and Stock Brokers.

title: 'Worth a tremendous amount of money': Trump claims Iran gave US 'big oil-and-gas-related present'
content: President Trump announced Iran delivered a "very big present",  a significant oil and gas related concession, amid ongoing conflict negotiations. He described it as a "tremendous amount of money" and a "very significant prize" potentially linked to the Strait of Hormuz, signaling optimism for a deal despite Iranian denials of talks.

title: Congress gets notice to move out of Lutyens address
content: The Congress party is set to lose its long-standing national headquarters at 24, Akbar Road. An eviction notice has also been issued for the Indian Youth Congress office at 5, Raisina Road. Both premises must be vacated by March 28. The party intends to challenge the notices in court, citing illegality and political motivation.

title: Harish Rana, India's first passive euthanasia case, cremated in Delhi
content: Harish Rana, India's first passive euthanasia recipient, has passed away at 31 after a 13-year coma. His cremation in Delhi marked a quiet end to a case that ignited national debate on life, dignity, and medical care.  The Supreme Court had permitted the withdrawal of life support, allowing for a natural death with palliative care, concluding his family's decade-long struggle.

title: 'They cannot have a nuclear weapon': US pushes 15-point plan to end Iran war, sent via Pak
content: The US has presented a 15-point proposal to Iran via Pakistan, seeking an end to the Middle East conflict. This diplomatic initiative addresses Iran's nuclear and missile programs and maritime security, even as military actions persist. Pakistan is actively mediating, offering to host talks, while Iran's response remains uncertain amidst internal challenges.

title: Iran wants to negotiate with JD Vance, rejects Kushner, Witkoff: Who’s involved in peace talks?
content: President Trump announced ongoing negotiations with Iran, involving several top officials including Jared Kushner and JD Vance. While Trump expressed optimism for a deal, Iranian officials denied any talks, accusing the US of spreading fake news. Iran reportedly prefers negotiating with Vance, viewing him as more inclined to end the conflict.

title: Actors who rejected Akshaye Khanna’s Dhurandhar role, now regret it: Mukesh Chhabra
content: Fresh off the thunderous success of Dhurandhar 2: The Revenge, casting director Mukesh Chhabra is soaking in what he calls a defining phase in his journey. “It took me 20 years to reach here, after doing over 300 films,” Chhabra told Hollywood Reporter India, reflecting on the film’s impact on his career.

title: Rupee falls 20 paise to 93.96 per US dollar in early trade
content: The Indian rupee continued its downward trend, falling to 93.96 against the US dollar amid persistent foreign fund outflows and global crude oil price hikes. Forex traders attribute the sustained weakness to uncertainty surrounding the West Asia crisis. Despite the rupee's decline, Indian stock markets showed strength, with benchmark indices trading higher.

title: Facing legal charges, Yash Dayal won't be part of IPL
content: Royal Challengers Bengaluru's Director of Cricket, Mo Bobat, confirmed that pacer Yash Dayal will not participate in the upcoming IPL season due to personal circumstances and ongoing legal issues.  Dayal, who missed the domestic season, remains under contract, but his absence is deemed best for both him and the franchise.  Bowling depth is further tested by Josh Hazlewood's recovery.

title: Bills' massive bison statues anchor Highmark Stadium's Family Circle in new renderings
content: the bison statues and the Family Circle are a part of an area that will be a first welcome to the new stadium . the installation will feature three stainless steel bison -- a bull, cow and calf -- modeled after the American bison .

title: Week 7 Top 25 and how to watch
content: the longhorns have won their past 26 games -- their only loss of the season came in early february . the other biggest mover in the top 10 was Florida, which won two of its three games against then-No. 1 Tennessee last week . there are no more undefeateds in the poll, with Grand Canyon losing its first game last Tuesday .

title: What returning production looks like for the 2026 college football season
content: the more continuity and experience you return from last season, the more likely you are to improve . it's still better to return a lot of your production than a little, even if last year's headliner bombed . a combination of injuries, tactical stagnation and disappointing development held back the Tigers .

title: 'A litmus test for the system': $50 million rosters and Nebraska's big challenge
content: the house settlement established a set of rules for paying players . it was intended to prevent the top programs from spending at a pace that made it impossible for the 100-plus other schools to compete . but those top programs have aggressively pursued loopholes to build rosters that are often worth double the money schools are allowed to share directly with their players. now, those rules are facing their first real test .
